mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) signals expanding real economic activity, which increases demand for capital and pushes up the 10-year Treasury yield as investors price in higher future growth and inflation.
failure_condition: If over a 3-year rolling window, the 20-day forward yield change following INDPRO increases (monthly) is not significantly positive (t-test p>0.10) or the hit rate is below 50%, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate (UNRATE) drops below 6.0% from elevated levels, it indicates a tightening labor market that compels the Federal Reserve to raise short-term rates, dragging up long-term yields via the expectation channel.
failure_condition: If in all instances where UNRATE crosses below 6.0%, the subsequent 20-day yield change does not have a median positive return > 0 bps, the hypothesis fails.

mechanism_narrative: A negative real fed funds rate (FEDFUNDS minus CPI inflation) forces investors to demand higher nominal yields on long-term Treasuries to compensate for inflation erosion, especially when inflation is already elevated. The joint behavior of FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL determines the expected real return.
failure_condition: If the rank IC of the composite real rate signal (FEDFUNDS minus trailing 12m CPI change) with 20-day yield changes is below 0.03 over any 2-year period, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: In a regime of near-zero short-term policy rates (FEDFUNDS < 1%), rising housing starts (HOUST) signal residential investment strength and economic momentum, which pushes up long-term yields as investors anticipate higher neutral rates.
failure_condition: If in the low-rate regime (FEDFUNDS<1%), the 60-day forward yield change is not positively correlated (spearman > 0.1) with HOUST releases, the hypothesis fails.

mechanism_narrative: Short-term increases in industrial production (INDPRO) are quickly reflected in bond yields as markets reprice near-term growth expectations, leading to a positive drift in the 10-year yield over the next week.
failure_condition: If the 5-day forward yield change following INDPRO releases has a negative mean return over a 5-year rolling window, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: When monthly retail and food services sales (RSAFS) exceed $430 billion, it indicates strong consumer spending, prompting expectations of tighter monetary policy and leading to a rise in the 10-year yield over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: If after each RSAFS release exceeding 430,000, the following 5-day yield change is negative more than 60% of the time, the hypothesis fails.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous strong payroll growth (PAYEMS increasing) and falling unemployment (UNRATE decreasing) create a tight labor market signal that forces the Federal Reserve to tighten policy, raising the entire yield curve. The joint trajectory of these two series is more informative than either alone.
failure_condition: If the composite labor tightness index (z-score PAYEMS increase minus z-score UNRATE change) shows no significant positive correlation (rank IC < 0.05) with 60-day yield changes over a 5-year window, reject.

mechanism_narrative: During an inflation surge regime (CPIAUCSL level above 236), further increases in the CPI index drive expectations of sustained high inflation, causing the 10-year Treasury yield to climb as term premiums rise.
failure_condition: If in all periods where CPIAUCSL > 236, the 20-day forward yield change has a median of zero or negative, the hypothesis fails.
